text: This chapter introduces the structural framework for Phase III by establishing that line studies ascend the fingerboard in successive pairs while maintaining fixed chord progressions (shared by letter designation), with each pair anchored to numbered forms of activity in G minor, creating a systematic method to hear harmonic changes across different neck positions. The pedagogical innovation includes cyclically extensible studies marked with continuation arrows and student-completed studies (5A/5B) that convert passive practice into active composition. The chapter's load-bearing concept is chord substitution—specifically the equivalences table showing how complex chords (maj6, maj7, maj9, 7, 13) reduce to minor forms (relative minor, secondary relative minor)—which serves as the mechanism enabling the method's linear improvisation approach. The five-step reduction procedure (study original form, learn voicings and substitutions, memorize through analysis, play sample solos, compose original solos) provides the complete workflow, and the chapter concludes by directing students to apply these substitution principles to canonical jazz standards like "Stella by Starlight" and "All the Things You Are," bridging technique exercise to real-world repertoire.
